compiled_state.title: As a new project member, I am asked about preplanning preferences in natural language instead of a clinical true/false prompt
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Replace the existing preplanning.interactive true/false question with natural-language phrasing and add answer normalization
**Why**: The current "Should preplanning run interactively? (true/false, default: true)" is clinical and confusing for non-technical users
**Scope**:
- IN: Natural-language rephrasing, boolean normalization before config write, yes/no follow-up for ambiguous responses, respect existing config on re-run
- OUT: Changing what the preplanning.interactive config key does downstream

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the preplanning question uses natural-language phrasing instead of true/false
  ← Satisfies: "The preplanning interactive question uses natural-language phrasing"
- When this story is complete, the user's answer is normalized to a boolean before writing to dso-config.conf, with ambiguous responses clarified via a yes/no follow-up
  ← Satisfies: "the answer is normalized to a boolean (true/false) before writing to dso-config.conf"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Must respect existing preplanning.interactive value on re-run (merge-not-overwrite concern)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Done definition update (adversarial review): The preplanning interactivity probe lives in Phase 3 Step 2c (Infrastructure Initialization), NOT Phase 2c. Phase 2 is the Socratic Dialogue. Implementers must modify the question in Phase 3 Step 2c of SKILL.md (~line 1154).
comments.author: Test
